cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is about understanding what it means if a person is "bitten by the travel bug." The passage provided talks about how some people are drawn to travel and explore the world. The options are A through D. First, I need to look at the context in the passage. It says, "While some people prefer to stay in the comfort of their own home, others been bitten by the travel bug and can't wait to explore the world." So the phrase "bitten by the travel bug" is contrasted with staying at home. The ones who are bitten can't wait to explore, which means they are eager to see the world. Looking at the options: A) prefers to stay at home – this is the opposite of what the travel bug is, so A is not correct. B) feels a lot for pain – the passage doesn't mention pain at all. "Travel bug" is a metaphor here, not a literal bug that causes pain. So B is probably incorrect. C) is eager to see the world – the passage says those bitten by the travel
